title: arabis drummondii var. oxyphylla
intro: Arabis drummondii var. oxyphylla, also known as Drummond's rockcress, is a perennial plant species in the mustard family (Brassicaceae) that is native to North America. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The health benefits of Arabis drummondii var. oxyphylla include its ability to aid in the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate pain. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by infusing the leaves and flowers in hot water to create a tea, and it can also be used topically as a poultice to aid in wound healing and skin conditions.
